arts.ttl: Report: Aston Villa increase bid for 'extremely fast and very dangerous' forward also wanted by Tottenham
arts.sum: Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur are both on the lookout for attacking reinforcements in the January transfer window. Neither the Villans nor Spurs are currently...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa transfer news: PSG contact revealed amid blockbuster Jhon Duran swap deal theory
arts.sum: However, after links with a switch to Chelsea and West Ham United this summer resulted in Duran not playing any pre-season friendlies, reputed interest from other would-be suitors has now resurfaced this month. Duran, 21, only signed a new contract three months ago which extends the player's deal until the summer of 2030.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa suffer fresh injury blow in West Ham clash ahead of Everton
arts.sum: Back-to-back away league games against Everton and Arsenal await next week, before Villa play in Monaco. Aston Villa have suffered a fresh injury blow in their FA Cup third round clash against West Ham.

arts.ttl: Roy Keane explains why he's not convinced by West Ham's appointment of Graham Potter to replace Julen Lopetegui
arts.sum: Roy Keane has issued a stark warning to newly-appointed West Ham boss Graham Potter as he scathed the Hammers hierarchy over their handling of former head coaches.

arts.ttl: Why Emi Martinez is missing from Aston Villa squad to face West Ham in FA Cup
arts.sum: Aston Villa have released a special commemorative kit to celebrate the club's 150th anniversary - and you can get your hands on one now. Gauci started both of Villa's Carabao Cup games against Wycombe Wanderers and Crystal Palace, but Olsen was injured at the time.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa team news vs West Ham confirmed as Rogers, Maatsen and Olsen start
arts.sum: Villa starting XI: Olsen, Cash, Konsa, Mings, Maatsen, Kamara, Barkley, Bailey, Tielemans, Rogers, Watkins. Unai Emery has named his Aston Villa team to take on West Ham in the FA Cup third round.

arts.ttl: The dream start! West Ham take early lead in Potter's first game LIVE!
arts.sum: Unai Emery has backed Graham Potter to succeed at West Ham ahead of their meeting with his Aston Villa side in the FA Cup . Speaking ahead of Friday's clash at Villa Park, Emery was asked about facing the new West Ham boss in his first game in charge of the Hammers.

Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. West Ham United - Fri, Jan 10th 2025, 20:00 Tyrone Mings captained Aston Villa for their FA Cup third-round tie against West Ham United at Villa Park. The centre-back wore the armband in the absence of John McGinn and Emi Martínez, as Unai Emery made three changes from last weekend’s Premier League victory over Leicester City. Robin Olsen, Ian Maatsen and Morgan Rogers were named in Villa’s starting XI, with the latter returning from a one-match suspension. Martínez was not named in the matchday 20 to face the Hammers, with McGinn injured, while Lucas Digne was included among the substitutes. He was joined by Academy graduate Bradley Burrowes, who took his place in Villa’s senior squad for the first time. The eagerly-anticipated evening saw the club continue its season-long celebrations of our 150th anniversary.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa v West Ham United: FA Cup third round – live
arts.sum: FA Cup updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off at Villa Park Live scoreboard | Read Football Daily | And mail Scott | 1 min: Just 41 seconds on the clock, in fact, and West Ham nearly take the lead in spectacular style. The ball’s shuttled right to left via Wan-Bissaka, Summerville and Paqueta, the latter pearling a diagonal rising shot from 25 yards that misses the top-right corner by an inch or two. Not sure Olsen was getting there. West Ham get the ball rolling. A reminder that this tie will be decided tonight, after extra time and penalty kicks if necessary. And there’s no VAR! Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Potter selects teenager Scarles in first West Ham team
arts.sum: West Ham United: Fabiański, Wan-Bissaka, Mavropanos, Kilman, Scarles, Álvarez, Souček ©, Paquetá, Kudus, Summerville, Füllkrug The substitutes’ bench will be comprised of goalkeeper Wes Foderingham, defenders Vladimír Coufal, Aaron Cresswell and Scarles’ fellow FA Youth Cup winner Kaelan Casey, midfielders Guido Rodríguez, Andy Irving, Carlos Soler and Luis Guilherme, and striker Danny Ings.
